title: fraxinus pennsylvanica subsp. oregona
intro: Fraxinus pennsylvanica subsp. oregona, also known as the Oregon green ash, is a subspecies of the green ash tree (Fraxinus pennsylvanica) native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. It is a deciduous tree with medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are derived from its bark, leaves, and roots. The health benefits of the Oregon green ash include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function, due to its high concentrations of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. The plant is traditionally used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
